source_jobs.title: Tax Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Join Vetcove and help modernize the future of veterinary software and the pet parent healthcare experience. Our suite of platforms features a market-leading procurement marketplace, an ultra-modern home delivery ecommerce experience, and a next-generation practice management system. More than 25,000 hospitals across all 50 states rely on Vetcove daily, supporting tens of thousands of veterinarians who care for tens of millions of pets each year.



We are modernizing the 50 billion dollar plus animal health industry by giving veterinary organizations the tools to focus more on patient care. Backed by Y Combinator and top venture investors in Silicon Valley, New York and Miami, Vetcove is a fast-growing, mission-driven company. We are searching for talented and passionate people who want to help shape the future of veterinary care.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Tax Specialist to join our team. In this role, you will oversee sales tax compliance (using Avalara), support income tax organization, and assist with tax planning activities. You’ll collaborate closely with internal accounting staff and external CPAs to ensure timely and accurate filings while optimizing our overall tax position.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Manage sales tax compliance processes using Avalara, including:
   
   - Monitoring nexus thresholds
   
   - Ensuring accurate tax rates on transactions
   
   - Filing and remitting sales tax returns across multiple jurisdictions

 - Maintain and organize documentation for federal, state, and local income taxes

 - Prepare and compile data packages for external accountants and tax advisors
   Support quarterly and annual tax provision processes
   Assist with tax planning strategies to reduce liabilities and ensure compliance

 - Monitor legislative and regulatory tax law developments; communicate impacts to leadership

 - Maintain tax calendars and ensure all deadlines are met

 - Support audits or inquiries from tax authorities




YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

 - 2+ years of experience in tax compliance, preferably with sales/use tax

 - Familiarity with Avalara or similar sales tax software strongly preferred

 - Strong understanding of U.S. tax regulations and compliance requirements

 - Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Strong communication skills to collaborate with internal teams and external accountants

 - Proficiency in Excel and general ledger/accounting software

 - CPA or working toward CPA designation

 - Experience in a multi-state tax environment

 - Exposure to tax planning or strategy projects




BENEFITS (FULL TIME ROLES)

 - 100% remote within the USA

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance

 - Automatic 401k contribution

 - Employee referral program

 - At home office set up

 - Bi-annual company retreats

 - Open vacation policy

 - Equity

 - Monthly team events



The typical base pay range for this role across the U.S. is: $60,000/year - $100,000/year + bonus + equity + benefits. There is a different range applicable to specific work locations. For Colorado, the salary range is: $65,000 - $85,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. This salary range is a good faith estimate of what Vetcove may pay for this position at the time of posting. Actual compensation may vary based on skills, qualifications, and experience. The range reflects the base hourly rate or annual compensation (as applicable), and does not include bonus, equity or other incentives.


In addition to base compensation, Vetcove offers a comprehensive benefits
package. Please refer to the “Benefits (Full Time Roles)” section above for more
details.



To apply, please submit your application through Vetcove’s Careers Page. The application deadline is accepted on an on going basis.



Please note we are unable to sponsor work visas at this time.

We are searching for talented and passionate people who want to help shape the future of veterinary care.



About the Position

The Associate General Counsel will be a key member of Vetcove’s legal team and a trusted partner to teams across the business. This role is ideal for an attorney who thrives in a high-growth startup environment, brings a strong work ethic and ownership mindset to every project, enjoys tackling problems big and small, and wants to help build the legal infrastructure for a rapidly expanding technology company operating in highly regulated markets.



You will work closely with Vetcove’s legal team, business leadership, product, and operations teams on a wide range of legal matters, including commercial contracting, product counseling, regulatory analysis, privacy, data security, intellectual property, and strategic growth initiatives. You should be excited to jump into new areas, master unfamiliar legal frameworks, and help the business move quickly while thoughtfully managing risk.



About our Team

Vetcove’s legal team is deeply embedded in the business and works cross-functionally across nearly every major company initiative. Our suite of platforms features a market-leading procurement marketplace, an ultra-modern home delivery ecommerce experience, and a next-generation practice management system. More than 25,000 hospitals across all 50 states rely on Vetcove daily, supporting tens of thousands of veterinarians who care for tens of millions of pets each year.



We are modernizing the 50 billion dollar plus animal health industry by giving veterinary organizations the tools to focus more on patient care. Backed by Y Combinator and top venture investors in Silicon Valley, New York and Miami, Vetcove is a fast-growing, mission-driven company. We are searching for talented and passionate people who want to help shape the future of veterinary care.

 

About the Position

 

As a Senior Product Manager at Vetcove, you’ll be at the heart of enhancing and scaling the platforms that power veterinary clinics and their pet parents. You will own high-impact product initiatives end-to-end, from defining strategy and roadmap to execution and post-launch optimization. You will drive cross-functional alignment, lead complex initiatives, and proactively identify opportunities to improve user experience, operational efficiency, and long-term platform scalability. You will monitor product performance and feedback, oversee robust quality assurance processes, and remove roadblocks that impact customer growth and retention. You will work closely with internal team members including account managers, onboarding associates, engineering, data, design, and support. You will also build strong relationships with external stakeholders, including veterinary clinics and pet parents. You will play a critical role in defining product vision, evaluating tradeoffs, and assessing strengths, opportunities, and risks associated with software products and services.

This isn’t just another product role—it’s an opportunity to shape the long-term strategy of a platform trusted by thousands of veterinary professionals. If you're passionate about solving meaningful challenges at scale, leading cross-functional teams, and delivering measurable business impact, we’d love to have you on board.

 

About Our Team

 

We’re a fast-moving team solving big challenges in an industry rapidly embracing technology. Our platform powers the day-to-day operations of veterinary clinics, from medical records to online shopping, but our mission goes beyond software—we’re creating a better, more efficient way for clinics to serve their patients

 

As a Product Manager, you’ll work closely with engineers, onboarding specialists, and support teams to bridge the gap between product and users. Your insights will drive improvements, ensuring our platform evolves to meet the ever-changing needs of veterinary clinics and pet parents

 

What You’ll Do

 - Drive Product Strategy & Roadmap: Define and own product strategy for key areas of the platform, translating company goals and customer insights into a clear, prioritized roadmap.

 - Lead Complex Initiatives: Oversee large, cross-functional projects from discovery through launch and iteration, ensuring on-time delivery and measurable impact.

 - Turn Feedback & Data into Action: Synthesize qualitative feedback and quantitative data to identify opportunities, validate hypotheses, and inform strategic decisions.

 - Optimize Product Performance: Establish and monitor KPIs, analyze product performance trends, and lead initiatives to improve adoption, engagement, retention, and revenue outcomes.

 - Strengthen Cross-Functional Alignment: Partner with engineering, design, data, onboarding, and support to align on requirements, scope, tradeoffs, and success metrics.

 - Elevate Product Documentation & Processes: Create detailed product requirement documents, user stories, and technical specifications to ensure clarity and execution excellence.

About the Role

As a Veterinary Project Manager at Vetcove, you'll serve as the bridge between our Product team and our veterinary customers. Leveraging your experience managing a veterinary hospital and working within a software company, you'll help ensure we're building products that solve operational challenges for clinics.

You'll partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, Customer Success, and external partners to bring real world veterinary expertise into the product development process. From advising on clinic workflows and managing strategic initiatives to coordinating product launches and serving as the primary point of contact for large hospital consolidators, you'll play a critical role in helping our Product team move quickly and build products our customers love.

This is an ideal role for someone who enjoys collaborating across teams, thrives in a fast paced environment, and is passionate about improving the veterinary industry through technology.



What You'll Do

 - Advise on Veterinary Workflows: Partner with product managers to provide expertise on veterinary clinic operations and workflows, helping validate product decisions and reduce unnecessary user research.

 - Lead Cross Functional Projects: Coordinate strategic projects across Product, Engineering, Customer Success and Operations, ensuring projects stay organized and on track from planning through execution.

 - Manage External Partnerships: Serve as the primary product contact for corporate veterinary groups, integration partners, and other third parties, leading discussions, gathering feedback, and driving collaborative initiatives.

 - Support Product Launches: Partner with product managers and cross functional teams to coordinate GTM activities, stakeholder communications, launch readiness, and post launch execution.

 - Bridge Business & Product Teams: Act as the liaison between product and business teams, helping align priorities, facilitate communication, and drive cross-functional initiatives forward.

 - Coordinate Product Operations: Track project timelines, manage dependencies, identify risks, and keep stakeholders informed to ensure successful execution of key initiatives.

 - Maintain Product Documentation: Create and maintain project plans, process documentation, meeting notes, and internal resources that keep cross functional teams aligned.

 - Champion the Customer Perspective: Leverage firsthand veterinary experience to ensure product decisions reflect real clinic workflows and deliver meaningful value to veterinary teams.



What We're Looking For

 - Bachelor's degree required

 - Experience managing operations at a veterinary hospital or clinic

 - Experience working at a software or technology company strongly preferred

 - Strong understanding of veterinary clinic operations, workflows, and practice management systems

 - Experience leading projects or coordinating cross functional initiatives involving multiple stakeholders

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills with exceptional attention to detail

 - Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast
